layman_headline: Extreme fear grips the market — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is deeply fearful with a Fear & Greed score of just 22, and Bitcoin just lost $424M from ETFs. While BTC is showing a small bounce today, most altcoins are still in downtrends with bearish chart structures. None of the swing candidates present a clean setup with good risk/reward and confirmed technical signals. Jumping in now without a strong catalyst would be catching a falling knife.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market is fearful but hasn't shown clear reversal signals yet. Watch for a confirmed bounce with volume before committing capital.
expert_reasoning: Scanning all seven candidates reveals no high-conviction setup. JTO has the best chart structure (slightly bullish, above SMA20) but RSI at 59 is elevated and the doji candle signals indecision — not a strong entry. CRV's 3.86x risk/reward is attractive but the strong downtrend (bias -0.95, below all MAs) makes it a knife-catch. GRASS's 29% weekly decline could mean-revert but chart bias is -0.45 with poor R/R at 0.69x. MON shows a bullish marubozu but R/R is only 0.79x and the overall structure is bearish. With BTC ETFs losing $424M, U.S.-Iran tensions escalating, and BTC dominance at 56.1% crushing alts, the macro backdrop does not support aggressive alt entries. Extreme Fear at 22 is historically a buy zone but requires confirmation — the trend is still 'improving' from lower levels, suggesting we're not at capitulation yet.
risk_factors: U.S.-Iran escalation could trigger further risk-off selling across all crypto assets
Bitcoin ETF outflows of $424M signal institutional de-risking that could accelerate
BTC dominance at 56.1% and rising means alt capital rotation is unlikely near-term
watch_list: Bitcoin reaction to CPI data and Warsh testimony — a dovish surprise could flip sentiment quickly
ETH strength (+4.9% 24h, +4.4% 7d) — if ETH confirms a breakout it could lead an alt rotation
Fear & Greed index — watch for a move above 30 to confirm sentiment turning
market_context: The market is in Extreme Fear (22/100) with BTC at $63,767 showing modest recovery but facing macro headwinds from geopolitical tensions and massive ETF outflows. High BTC dominance at 56.1% continues to suppress altcoin performance.
context_banner: Extreme Fear reigns with no confirmed reversal — disciplined patience outperforms forced entries.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 22/100 with improving trend suggests we're in a potential accumulation zone, but no capitulation spike yet to confirm a bottom.
Bitcoin ETFs lost $424M, completely wiping out prior week's inflows — institutional money is leaving, a major bearish headwind.
U.S.-Iran escalation weighing on bitcoin and stocks as oil climbs, adding geopolitical risk premium to an already fragile market.
U.S. June CPI fell 0.4%, potentially easing hawkish Fed pressure — a positive longer-term signal but not yet priced in.
Most swing candidates show neutral RSI (45-59 range) — no deep oversold readings to trigger high-probability mean-reversion entries.
